mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in RSAFS signals accelerating household demand and near-term revenue growth for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the retail-sales trend is rising.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS crossing below 0.25% identifies an emergency-easing liquidity regime that lowers discount rates and supports equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered zero-rate observations have lower mean 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if the triggered hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, investors price future policy tightening and lower real purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value is 261.775 and FEDFUNDS is 0.09
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not produce lower 20-day returns than either high CPIAUCSL growth alone or low FEDFUNDS alone across rolling 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in INDPRO and PAYEMS over 3 months identify a real-activity recovery in which output and employment expand together, raising earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both increase over the latest 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value is 105.7244 and PAYEMS is 142624.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-PAYEMS expansion trigger has a 60-day return hit rate below 50% or if its mean return is not higher than observations where only one series is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A HOUST decline greater than 10% over 3 months from an elevated construction level signals weakening housing demand and future cyclical slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 3 months after being above 1500
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers are followed by nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if triggered observations do not underperform the unconditional 60-day return.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation trend crossing above its 12-month average signals a discount-rate and margin-pressure shock, causing investors to de-risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth crosses above its 12-month average growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL upside-trend crossings are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered cases or if average triggered returns exceed baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 signals broad real demand expansion that lifts aggregate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days despite publication lag.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth observations fail to generate higher mean 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative GDPC1 growth observations or if rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.
